steps.title: Overview: Printable Kit Generation
steps.description: The assembly tour showed how flat parts become a previewed enclosure. Printable generation uses the same domain facts to make physical print parts, split oversized panels, create glue keys and connectors, lay parts onto beds, and build 3MF packages.

steps.title: Print Volume Presets
steps.description: Print volume presets model bounded and unbounded beds. The selected preset controls whether parts must be split and how print sheets are arranged.

steps.title: Printable Part Type
steps.description: A printable part carries kind, dimensions, mesh, source panel id, cut feature count, and optional label. This is more than a display object; it is the export unit.

steps.title: Base Kit Factory
steps.description: The base kit factory receives a layout and bed preset, then creates the generated Nukit print kit. Specialized designs use the same kit contract through their own generators.

steps.title: Design Router
steps.description: The print design router chooses Corsi, donut, or generated Nukit kit creation from the active layout. This keeps UI export code from knowing every model-specific generator.

steps.title: Sheet Plan
steps.description: Sheet planning groups printable parts onto print beds. The resulting plan is used by SVG previews, Three.js print-sheet previews, and 3MF plate metadata.

steps.title: Print Sheet SVG
steps.description: The older SVG print-sheet view is a lightweight formatting layer over the same sheet plan. It does not decide part sizes or split strategy.

steps.title: 3MF Export From Kit
steps.description: 3MF export from a printable kit reuses the part meshes and sheet plan. It packages the generated data without depending on rendered canvas state.

steps.title: Bed Fit Tests
steps.description: The Corsi tests assert that generated print parts fit bounded beds and that panel areas remain accounted for. These tests are a useful map of printable geometry assumptions.

steps.title: Quick Check
steps.text: Why does the app create a printable sheet plan before rendering print sheets?
answers: The plan is shared by SVG, Three.js preview, and 3MF plate placement
The plan replaces the need for mesh geometry
The plan stores user passwords for slicer upload
The plan is only needed for laser SVG export
steps.explanation: `createPrintableSheetPlanFromKit` starts at line 324 in `src/printableKit.ts`, and the plan is then consumed by preview and export paths.

steps.title: Summary
steps.description: Printable generation creates typed parts, meshes, sheet placements, and package data from the layout. The next tour covers the specialized print designs that plug into this same contract.
